outtro.p:
Note that the summands of \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle \left( \frac{ {{num0}} }{ {{dem0}} } \right) + \left( \frac{ {{num1}} }{ {{dem1}} } \right) + \left( \frac{ {{num2}} }{ {{dem2}} } \right) +\left( \frac{ {{num3}} }{ {{dem3}} } \right) +\left( \frac{ {{num4}} }{ {{dem4}} } \right) +\cdots
follows the pattern of \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle {{Ax}} where x={{Input}} and n starts at {{k0}}.

Since \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle {{fx}} = \sum_{ n = {{k0}} }^\infty {{ax}}, we have that \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle F(x) = {{Fx}} = \sum_{ n = {{k0}} }^\infty {{Ax}}. So \renewcommand{\log}{\ln}\displaystyle \left( \frac{ {{num0}} }{ {{dem0}} } \right) + \left( \frac{ {{num1}} }{ {{dem1}} } \right) + \left( \frac{ {{num2}} }{ {{dem2}} } \right) +\left( \frac{ {{num3}} }{ {{dem3}} } \right) +\left( \frac{ {{num4}} }{ {{dem4}} } \right) +\cdots = F\left({{Input}}\right) = {{Limit}}.
